title: Allow podman to read exit codes of removed containers
body:
We can read the exit file created by conmon to get the exit code instead of querying libpod.

Also, do not error on cleanup if the container is already gone, as a completely removed container is definitely cleaned up.

Resolves: #527

comments.body:
:pushpin: Commit fc85bf0 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan fc85bf0868e5043c4ee5f1965514d4eefdfe479a -->
CRI-O uses the same mechanism, and they don't seem to have problems, so I'm not terribly worried.
Aren't these on /run, so they get culled at reboot.

Yep, they're on /run and get culled on reboot.  I'm just a little worried about a machine that stays up for a year with thousands of containers getting created daily.   Just something to keep in the back of the mind.
Yup, there are potentially lots of issues with that in /run. But CRI-O is much more likely to cause that then podman would.
